```Fortran
	SUBROUTINE HELLO
	PRINT *, 'Hello'
	END
```
and the latter being:
```Fortran
	PROGRAM MAIN
	CALL HELLO()
	END
```

I get an error when compiling the second file into an object:
```
$ lfortran -c main.f 
semantic error: Function 'hello' not found or not implemented yet (if it is intrinsic)
 --> main.f:2:2
  |
2 |  CALL HELLO()
  |  ^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
```
while `gfortran` eats that fine.

Any suggestions?

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281","id":2113577941,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc59-qPV","number":3281,"title":"linking a shared library","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-01T22:02:46Z","updated_at":"2024-02-01T22:02:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I am trying to link a shared library.
```
lfortran -v -fPIC --generate-object-code --implicit-interface -shared -Wl,-soname,libworld.so -o libworld.so world.f.o
```

We don't have `-shared` option, as this needs to go to `clang`, I thought
```
lfortran -v -fPIC --generate-object-code --implicit-interface -W,-shared -Wl,-soname,libworld.so -o libworld.so world.f.o
```
but that fails with:
```
lfortran -v -fPIC --generate-object-code --implicit-interface -W,-shared -Wl,-soname,libworld.so -olibworld.so world.f.o
clang -o libworld.so world.f.o  -L"/usr/bin/../lib64" -Wl,-rpath,"/usr/bin/../lib64" -llfortran_runtime -lm
/usr/bin/ld: /usr/bin/../lib/gcc/x86_64-redhat-linux/13/../../../../lib64/crt1.o: in function `_start':
(.text+0x1b): undefined reference to `main'
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
```
For some reason, the `-shared` is missing in the `clang` call.

Running:
```
clang -o libworld.so world.f.o  -L"/usr/bin/../lib64" -shared -Wl,-rpath,"/usr/bin/../lib64" -llfortran_runtime -lm
```
works, so somewhere there is a bug in the `-W` option parsing....","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3281/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```

We don't have `-shared` option, as this needs to go to `clang`, I thought
```
lfortran -v -fPIC --generate-object-code --implicit-interface -W,-shared -Wl,-soname,libworld.so -o libworld.so world.f.o
```
but that fails with:
```
lfortran -v -fPIC --generate-object-code --implicit-interface -W,-shared -Wl,-soname,libworld.so -olibworld.so world.f.o
clang -o libworld.so world.f.o  -L"/usr/bin/../lib64" -Wl,-rpath,"/usr/bin/../lib64" -llfortran_runtime -lm
/usr/bin/ld: /usr/bin/../lib/gcc/x86_64-redhat-linux/13/../../../../lib64/crt1.o: in function `_start':
(.text+0x1b): undefined reference to `main'
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
```
For some reason, the `-shared` is missing in the `clang` call.

Running:
```
clang -o libworld.so world.f.o  -L"/usr/bin/../lib64" -shared -Wl,-rpath,"/usr/bin/../lib64" -llfortran_runtime -lm
```

Implement or ignore?

GNU does:
```
       -rdynamic
           Pass  the  flag  -export-dynamic  to  the  ELF linker, on targets that support it. This instructs the linker to add all
           symbols, not only used ones, to the dynamic symbol table. This option is needed for some uses of "dlopen" or  to  allow
           obtaining backtraces from within a program.
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3310/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3310/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Implement or ignore?

GNU does:
```
       -rdynamic
           Pass  the  flag  -export-dynamic  to  the  ELF linker, on targets that support it. This instructs the linker to add all
           symbols, not only used ones, to the dynamic symbol table. This option is needed for some uses of "dlopen" or  to  allow
           obtaining backtraces from within a program.
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3310/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3310/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3336","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3336/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3336/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3336/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3336","id":2119775672,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc5-WTW4","number":3336,"title":"multiple definition","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-06T01:45:12Z","updated_at":"2024-02-06T01:45:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Related to #3208 

Consider two files:
```
module first
  interface inner
  end interface inner
end module first

module second
  REAL :: C = 1
end module second
```
and
```
PROGRAM MAINF90
  use second
  PRINT *,'Constant is',C
END PROGRAM MAINF90
```

If I compile them both at the same time:
```
$ lfortran file2.f90 file1.f90
Constant is 1.00000000e+00
```
It works. But compiling them into objects and then linking them fails:
```
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file1.f90
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file2.f90
$ lfortran fiile2.o file1.o
/usr/bin/ld: file1.o:(.data+0x0): multiple definition of `c'; file2.o:(.data+0x0): first defined here
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o file2.out file2.o file1.o  -L"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
```

```Fortran
	SUBROUTINE HELLO
	PRINT *, 'Hello'
	END
```
and the latter being:
```Fortran
	PROGRAM MAIN
	CALL HELLO()
	END
```

I get an error when compiling the second file into an object:
```
$ lfortran -c main.f 
semantic error: Function 'hello' not found or not implemented yet (if it is intrinsic)
 --> main.f:2:2
  |
2 |  CALL HELLO()
  |  ^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
```
while `gfortran` eats that fine.

Any suggestions?

ion":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Related to #3208 

Consider two files:
```
module first
  interface inner
  end interface inner
end module first

module second
  REAL :: C = 1
end module second
```
and
```
PROGRAM MAINF90
  use second
  PRINT *,'Constant is',C
END PROGRAM MAINF90
```

If I compile them both at the same time:
```
$ lfortran file2.f90 file1.f90
Constant is 1.00000000e+00
```
It works. But compiling them into objects and then linking them fails:
```
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file1.f90
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file2.f90
$ lfortran fiile2.o file1.o
/usr/bin/ld: file1.o:(.data+0x0): multiple definition of `c'; file2.o:(.data+0x0): first defined here
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o file2.out file2.o file1.o  -L"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
```

ion":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Related to #3208 

Consider two files:
```
module first
  interface inner
  end interface inner
end module first

module second
  REAL :: C = 1
end module second
```
and
```
PROGRAM MAINF90
  use second
  PRINT *,'Constant is',C
END PROGRAM MAINF90
```

If I compile them both at the same time:
```
$ lfortran file2.f90 file1.f90
Constant is 1.00000000e+00
```
It works. But compiling them into objects and then linking them fails:
```
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file1.f90
$ lfortran --generate-object-code  -c file2.f90
$ lfortran fiile2.o file1.o
/usr/bin/ld: file1.o:(.data+0x0): multiple definition of `c'; file2.o:(.data+0x0): first defined here
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o file2.out file2.o file1.o  -L"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
```

`test_module_main.f90`:
```Fortran
PROGRAM MAINF90
  USE TEST_MODULE, only : TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION
  PRINT *,'Sum is',TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION(1., 2.)
END PROGRAM MAINF90
```
`test_module_implementation.f90`:
```Fortran
FUNCTION TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION(A,B)
  REAL :: TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION
  REAL, INTENT(IN) :: A
  REAL, INTENT(IN) :: B
  TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION = A + B
END FUNCTION TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION
```
and  `test_module_interface.f90`:
```
MODULE TEST_MODULE
  INTERFACE
     FUNCTION TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION(A,B)
       REAL :: TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION
       REAL, INTENT(IN) :: A
       REAL, INTENT(IN) :: B
     END FUNCTION TEST_MODULE_FUNCTION
  END INTERFACE
END MODULE TEST_MODULE
```

```
lfortran -c test_module_main.f90 
lfortran -c test_module_implementation.f90 
lfortran -c test_module_interface.f90
lfortran test_module_main.o test_module_implementation.o test_module_interface.o -o test_module
/usr/bin/ld: test_module_main.o: in function `main':
LFortran:(.text+0x1e): undefined reference to `test_module_function'
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
The command 'clang -o test_module test_module_main.o test_module_implementation.o test_module_interface.o  -L"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -Wl,-rpath,"/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/src/bin/../runtime" -llfortran_runtime -lm' failed.
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1931030436/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

@aaronpuchert as you are the openSUSE guy, any ideas?

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","id":2065953825,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc57I_Qh","number":3042,"title":"Location of lfortran runtime library `.mod` files","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-01-04T16:22:26Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T17:16:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"After the recent changes, here is how conda-forge installs the runtime library (https://github.com/conda-forge/lfortran-feedstock/pull/73):
```console
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran/kernel.json
-- Installing: $PREFIX/bin/lfortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime_static.a
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.30.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/lfortran_intrinsics.h
'$BUILD_PREFIX/bin/cmake' '--install' '$SRC_DIR/build/src/runtime'
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_builtin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_custom.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_optimization.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_kind.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_fortran_env.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_ieee_arithmetic.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math2.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math3.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_trig.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_sin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_string.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_bit.mod
-- Done.
```
So:
* the `liblfortran_runtime` static and dynamic library goes into `$PREFIX/lib` (I think that's fine)
* The corresponding header file `lfortran_intrinsics.h` goes into `$PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/` (that's probably ok for now)
* The `*.mod` files go into `$PREFIX/lib/`. I think that's problematic, I think `.mod` files do not belong there. I think that should go into `$PREFIX/share/lfortran` or something like that.

@junghans where do you recommend conda stores the `.mod` files? I will also have to test the package by hand, I suspect things don't get found automatically.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1877476942","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042#issuecomment-1877476942","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","id":1877476942,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc5v6AZO","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-04T17:16:21Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T17:16:21Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I got inspired by the gfortran package on Fedora:

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","id":2065953825,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc57I_Qh","number":3042,"title":"Location of lfortran runtime library `.mod` files","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-01-04T16:22:26Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T20:01:56Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"After the recent changes, here is how conda-forge installs the runtime library (https://github.com/conda-forge/lfortran-feedstock/pull/73):
```console
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran/kernel.json
-- Installing: $PREFIX/bin/lfortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime_static.a
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.30.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/lfortran_intrinsics.h
'$BUILD_PREFIX/bin/cmake' '--install' '$SRC_DIR/build/src/runtime'
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_builtin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_custom.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_optimization.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_kind.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_fortran_env.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_ieee_arithmetic.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math2.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math3.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_trig.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_sin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_string.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_bit.mod
-- Done.
```
So:
* the `liblfortran_runtime` static and dynamic library goes into `$PREFIX/lib` (I think that's fine)
* The corresponding header file `lfortran_intrinsics.h` goes into `$PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/` (that's probably ok for now)
* The `*.mod` files go into `$PREFIX/lib/`. I think that's problematic, I think `.mod` files do not belong there. I think that should go into `$PREFIX/share/lfortran` or something like that.

@junghans where do you recommend conda stores the `.mod` files? I will also have to test the package by hand, I suspect things don't get found automatically.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1877686805","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042#issuecomment-1877686805","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","id":1877686805,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc5v6zoV","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-04T20:01:55Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T20:01:55Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"yeah, > * headers. Depend on compiler.
> * shared lib runtime, i.e. similar to `libgfortran` package, which contains just one file. This should be  a dependency for compiler.
> * static lib runtime, i.e. similar to `libgfortran-static` package, which contains just one file.
> * Jupiter file.  This would also require listing some Jupiter dependencies. Depends on compiler.

I am not a huge fan of splitting things into too many sub-packages, especially if it is for a single file. openSUSE requires shared libraries to be in separate packages, but fedora does not - I think.

For static libraries, they should be separate if package builds static and shared libraries:
https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/packaging-guidelines/#_packaging_static_libraries","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1880136870/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3042","id":2065953825,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc57I_Qh","number":3042,"title":"Location of lfortran runtime library `.mod` files","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-01-04T16:22:26Z","updated_at":"2024-01-11T00:20:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"After the recent changes, here is how conda-forge installs the runtime library (https://github.com/conda-forge/lfortran-feedstock/pull/73):
```console
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran/kernel.json
-- Installing: $PREFIX/bin/lfortran
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime_static.a
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.30.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.0.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/liblfortran_runtime.dylib
-- Installing: $PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/lfortran_intrinsics.h
'$BUILD_PREFIX/bin/cmake' '--install' '$SRC_DIR/build/src/runtime'
-- Install configuration: "Release"
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_builtin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_custom.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_optimization.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_kind.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_fortran_env.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_ieee_arithmetic.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_iso_c_binding.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math2.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math3.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_math.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_trig.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_sin.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_string.mod
-- Installing: $PREFIX/lib/lfortran_intrinsic_bit.mod
-- Done.
```
So:
* the `liblfortran_runtime` static and dynamic library goes into `$PREFIX/lib` (I think that's fine)
* The corresponding header file `lfortran_intrinsics.h` goes into `$PREFIX/include/lfortran/impure/` (that's probably ok for now)
* The `*.mod` files go into `$PREFIX/lib/`. I think that's problematic, I think `.mod` files do not belong there. I think that should go into `$PREFIX/share/lfortran` or something like that.

@junghans where do you recommend conda stores the `.mod` files? I will also have to test the package by hand, I suspect things don't get found Would you also consider to provide binaries for the major Linux distros?

it is great to see the developments of `lfortran`.
Would you also consider to provide binaries for the major Linux distros? 
It would be nice to have an official lfortran releases for Fedora/RHEL/SUSE in e.g. COPR https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/

Of course there are alredy some unofficial builds, e.g. https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/fed500/lfortran/, but it would be nice to have a blessed version from the developers.

Best regards,

```Dockerfile
FROM rockylinux:9

RUN dnf install -y epel-release
RUN dnf install "dnf-command(config-manager)"
RUN dnf config-manager --set-enabled crb
RUN dnf install -y fedpkg
RUN git clone --branch epel9 https://src.fedoraproject.org/forks/junghans/rpms/lfortran.git
WORKDIR lfortran
RUN dnf builddep -y lfortran.spec
RUN fedpkg local
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1907222420/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

` cat docker/Dockerfile`

```Dockerfile
FROM rockylinux:9

RUN dnf install -y epel-release
RUN dnf install "dnf-command(config-manager)"
RUN dnf config-manager --set-enabled crb
RUN dnf install -y fedpkg
RUN git clone --branch epel9 https://src.fedoraproject.org/forks/junghans/rpms/lfortran.git
WORKDIR lfortran
RUN dnf builddep -y lfortran.spec
RUN fedpkg local || true
```
and
```bash
docker build -t lfortran docker/
docker run -it lfortran /bin/bash
```
and then in the container:
```bash
. /opt/rh/gcc-toolset-13/enable
cd lfortran-0.31.0
cd redhat-linux-build
gdb ./src/lfortran/tests/test_lfortran
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1908581503/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Our preprocessor seems incomplete, `#elif` gets ignored, e.g.
```f90
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#ifdef __GNU__
#elif defined(__LFORTRAN__)
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#endif
```

And it cannot process CMake's compiler test file [CMakeFortranCompilerId.F]:(https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/files/14056118/CMakeFortranCompilerId.F.zip)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292","id":1338692951,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc5PytVX","number":292,"title":"Add __LFORTRAN__ / __LFORT__ predefined macro","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-08-15T08:27:21Z","updated_at":"2024-01-25T20:36:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"*Original issue*: https://gitlab.com/lfortran/lfortran/-/issues/261

This seems like the best path forward to be supported in https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/blob/5025b568433d6b7027b81822f1782defb80be74a/Modules/CMakeFortranCompilerId.F.in. (This relates to https://gitlab.com/lfortran/lfortran/-/issues/150.)

For comparison, `gcc` / `gfortran` has: https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc-4.4.7/cpp/Common-Predefined-Macros.html

(I am a little unsure of the current level of support for preprocessor directives in `lfortran`, based on reading of https://github.com/fortran-lang/stdlib/issues/35.)

E.g. a relevant section for `flang`

```
#elif defined(__FLANG)
        PRINT *, 'INFO:compiler[Flang]'
# define COMPILER_VERSION_MAJOR DEC(__FLANG_MAJOR__)
# define COMPILER_VERSION_MINOR DEC(__FLANG_MINOR__)
# if defined(__FLANG_PATCHLEVEL__)
#  define COMPILER_VERSION_PATCH DEC(__FLANG_PATCHLEVEL__)
# endif
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1910959278","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292#issuecomment-1910959278","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/292","id":1910959278,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc5x5uyu","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-25T20:36:35Z","updated_at":"2024-01-25T20:36:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Fixed in #2859 ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1910959278/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Our preprocessor seems incomplete, `#elif` gets ignored, e.g.
```f90
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#ifdef __GNU__
#elif defined(__LFORTRAN__)
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#endif
```

And it cannot process CMake's compiler test file [CMakeFortranCompilerId.F]:(https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/files/14056118/CMakeFortranCompilerId.F.zip)

Our preprocessor seems incomplete, `#elif` gets ignored, e.g.
```f90
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#ifdef __GNU__
#elif defined(__LFORTRAN__)
print *, __LFORTRAN__
#endif
```

And it cannot process CMake's compiler test file [CMakeFortranCompilerId.F]:(https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/files/14056118/CMakeFortranCompilerId.F.zip)

> 
> * [ ]  How is `CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_LIBDIR` set? Do we set it at the command line? If not, is it always set? It seems this is set by `include(GNUInstallDirs)`.
Yes, see https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/module/GNUInstallDirs.html, the `FULL` variable are constructed automatically.

> * [ ]   Consequently, the code `#ifdef CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_LIBDIR` always evaluates to true, so we should just remove the "else" condition. However that removes the relative paths.

I left it there in case one doesn't compile with cmake.
And the case for "installed" was already an absolute path before.

> * [ ]  We need to add some tests for this that fails in master. Otherwise this will get broken in the future. I am trying to figure out how to construct it.

Well we wanted to install the library etc on the right spots. That was just wrong before, only as a plus it is npw also configurable.

> * [ ]  I don't like setting environment variables at our CI, would like to figure out how to make things work automatically.

I will look into that.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1837345832/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

- One can set `CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR` more than one deep (`bin/bin` was broken before)

- libraries do not get install in share (arch independent directory) anymore
- Native Windows paths

- Using absolute `CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR` etc.

To test this, we indeed must add a CI test that fails if these relative paths are incorrect. Then we can test this by physically moving (relocating) the directory with lfortran, and ensure things still work (and fail if relative path is incorrect).\r\n\r\nLet me clarify, the CI of the 1st commit failed because a `/` was missing and then `liblfortran` could not be found during linking, so the CI covers `get_runtime_library_dir()`. But there was another typo in `get_runtime_library_header_dir()` which the CI never choked on, I would have guessed an error like \"`lfortran_intrinsics.h` could not be found\", would be triggered. So apparently we don't have a test that used an installed `lfortran` and `lfortran_intrinsics.h` (not sure which back-end uses it).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1854816503/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I played around with this:
```
./lfortran --backend=c ../../examples/expr2.f90
expr2.out.tmp.o.tmp.c:7:10: fatal error: lfortran_intrinsics.h: No such file or directory
    7 | #include <lfortran_intrinsics.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.
The command 'gcc  -I/home/christoph/computing/lfortran/inst/bin/../share/lpython/lib/impure -o expr2.out.tmp.o -c expr2.out.tmp.o.tmp.c' failed.
```

So it seems `--backend=c` is using `get_runtime_library_c_header_dir()` not `get_runtime_library_header_dir()`, so 1) we need to fix `get_runtime_library_c_header_dir()` as well and b) I still need a way to trigger `get_runtime_library_header_dir()` in a test.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/1858433335/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/comments/1428607766","pull_request_review_id":1785075391,"id":1428607766,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCpMuHc5VJtMW","diff_hunk":"@@ -46,6 +46,28 @@ endif(NOT WITH_XEUS)
 # For CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR:
 include(GNUInstallDirs)
 
+# for relocatable binaries we can support only relative paths
+foreach(_p BIN LIB INCLUDE)
+  cmake_path(IS_ABSOLUTE CMAKE_INSTALL_${_p}DIR IS_${p}_ABSOLUTE)
+  if(IS_${p}_ABSOLUTE)
+    message(FATAL_ERROR "CMAKE_INSTALL_${_p}DIR cannot be an absolute path")
+  endif()
+endforeach()
+
+# create CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE, LIBDIR relative to BINDIR
+# create CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE, INCLUDEDIR relative to BINDIR
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR} TO_CMAKE_PATH_LIST CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR NORMALIZE)
+string(REPLACE "/" ";" CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR_LIST "${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}")
+set(CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE "/")
+set(CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE "/")
+foreach(_p ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR_LIST})
+  string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE "../")
+  string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE "../")
+endforeach()
+string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR})
+string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR})
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE} TO_NATIVE_PATH_LIST CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE)
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE} TO_NATIVE_PATH_LIST CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE)","path":"CMakeLists.txt","commit_id":"ca132b3441a06807d4a92ad687472f3fa65d6760","original_commit_id":"ca132b3441a06807d4a92ad687472f3fa65d6760","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I didn't know that commands, great! {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/comments/1428609214","pull_request_review_id":1785076588,"id":1428609214,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCpMuHc5VJti-","diff_hunk":"@@ -46,6 +46,28 @@ endif(NOT WITH_XEUS)
 # For CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR:
 include(GNUInstallDirs)
 
+# for relocatable binaries we can support only relative paths
+foreach(_p BIN LIB INCLUDE)
+  cmake_path(IS_ABSOLUTE CMAKE_INSTALL_${_p}DIR IS_${p}_ABSOLUTE)
+  if(IS_${p}_ABSOLUTE)
+    message(FATAL_ERROR "CMAKE_INSTALL_${_p}DIR cannot be an absolute path")
+  endif()
+endforeach()
+
+# create CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE, LIBDIR relative to BINDIR
+# create CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE, INCLUDEDIR relative to BINDIR
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR} TO_CMAKE_PATH_LIST CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR NORMALIZE)
+string(REPLACE "/" ";" CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR_LIST "${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}")
+set(CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE "/")
+set(CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE "/")
+foreach(_p ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR_LIST})
+  string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE "../")
+  string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE "../")
+endforeach()
+string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR})
+string(APPEND CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR})
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE} TO_NATIVE_PATH_LIST CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR_RELATIVE)
+cmake_path(CONVERT ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR_RELATIVE} TO_NATIVE_PATH_LIST requested"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-05-17T16:16:43Z","updated_at":"2023-05-17T17:28:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Comment:

Hi, I noticed that the recipe builds the static library, rather than the shared as is common for conda: https://github.com/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/meta.yaml#L31-L32 . Was there a technical reason for this, or would you be open to a switch to shared?

For reference, I've got an outline for a shared lib recipe https://github.com/psi4/psi4meta/blob/master/conda-recipes/libecpint/build.sh#L34-L50 

A few other unimplemented plans I've had for ecpint:
* turn off xml by default
* use cerf instead of vendoring Faddeeva https://github.com/robashaw/libecpint/blob/master/src/CMakeLists.txt#L56-L78
* spliting c and py packages
* (as mentioned) use shared instead of static libs

I haven't looked at libecpint recently, but I thought I'd inquire, and see what you think and need. Thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/4/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/4/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/comments/1551797300","html_url":"https://github.com/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/4#issuecomment-1551797300","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/4","id":1551797300,"node_id":"IC_kwDOGfbxPs5cfow0","user":{"login":"junghans","id":439483,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzOTQ4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/439483?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans","html_url":"https://github.com/junghans","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junghans/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-17T17:28:30Z","updated_at":"2023-05-17T17:28:30Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Nope, we use shared everything else!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/libecpint-feedstock/issues/comments/1551797300/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
